name: Aaron
blurb: A handy Thunder Lake stopover on the Trans-Canada halfway between Thunder Bay and Winnipeg, split in two by Thunder Creek.
fishing: Thunder Lake: walleye, pike, muskie, perch

campgrounds.sub: Mixed electric · Site 44 has the lake view

name: Achray
blurb: On Grand Lake on the remote east side of the park, near the Barron Canyon. Rustic sites and cabins.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

campgrounds.sub: East side · Remote · Grand Lake · Cabins

name: Arrowhead
blurb: Three campgrounds near Huntsville, with Stubb's Falls, a swimmable lake, and a famous winter ice-skating trail.
fishing: Arrowhead and Mayflower Lakes: bass, pike, rainbow trout

campgrounds.sub: Electric, Cabins, Closest to Stubb's Falls
Electric, Large private sites, Near the beach

trails.name: Stubb's Falls Trail
trails.detail: Flat forest walk to the Little East River waterfall

trails.name: Big Bend Lookout
trails.detail: Short walk to a viewing deck over the Big East River gooseneck

trails.name: Mayflower Lake Trail
trails.detail: Boardwalks and climbs around a deep spring-fed lake

trails.name: Homesteaders Trail
trails.detail: Rugged terrain past abandoned 1870s farm fields

trails.name: Beaver Meadow Trail
trails.detail: Porcupine Bluffs, a beaver pond, and the old Oke homestead

name: Awenda
blurb: A forested peninsula on Georgian Bay with five beaches and six campgrounds shaded by sugar maples. Kettle's Lake is motor-free for paddling, and 30 km of trails wind to the shore.
fishing: Georgian Bay: bass, pike, walleye, panfish; Kettle's Lake: bass, panfish

campgrounds.sub: Radio-free and dog-free, Near the beaches

trails.name: Beaver Pond Trail
trails.detail: Barrier-free boardwalk loop through a beaver-shaped nature reserve

trails.name: Nipissing Trail
trails.detail: A 155-step staircase up the ancient Nipissing Bluff

trails.name: Robitaille Homestead Trail
trails.detail: Past an abandoned 1902 farmstead to 11,500-year-old dunes

trails.name: Beach Trail
trails.detail: Along the Georgian Bay shoreline with Giant's Tomb Island views

trails.name: Wendat Trail
trails.detail: Loops Kettle's Lake with a marsh boardwalk and lots of birds

trails.name: Bluff Trail
trails.detail: Long forested loop along the bluff linking the campgrounds and trails

name: Balsam Lake
blurb: A big family park on Balsam Lake, the high point of the Trent-Severn Waterway, with a long sandy beach and eight campgrounds. Good fishing for bass, walleye, and muskie.
fishing: Balsam Lake: largemouth and smallmouth bass, walleye, muskie

campgrounds.sub: Non-electric, Closest to the boat launch

trails.name: Lookout Trail
trails.detail: Through glacial eskers and kames to a panoramic lookout

trails.name: Lorie's Link Trail
trails.detail: Fields of wildflowers and a pine plantation

trails.name: Woodland Trail
trails.detail: A forest trek with both natural and pioneer highlights

trails.name: Pond Loop Trail
trails.detail: Circles a pond full of aquatic life

name: Bass Lake
blurb: A warm, shallow spring lake minutes from Orillia. An easy first-trip park with a big groomed beach and Prospector tents to rent.
fishing: Bass Lake: largemouth bass, perch, pike

campgrounds.sub: Smallest · Non-electric · Closest to the water

trails.name: Lakeshore Trail
trails.detail: Shoreline, forest, and the 1830s Rowe homestead; partly paved

name: Batchawana Bay
blurb: A warm, shallow sweep of Lake Superior sand where the highway meets the bay. Picnic, swim, watch the lake. Day use only.
fishing: Lake Superior: trout and salmon nearby

name: Blue Lake
blurb: Water so clear it looks tropical, an 800 metre sand beach, and a climbing crag across the lake. The northwest's beach park.
fishing: Blue Lake: lake trout, pike, smallmouth bass

name: Bon Echo
blurb: Home to the 100 m Mazinaw Rock and its Indigenous pictographs, reached by the Mugwump ferry. Two lakeside campgrounds plus walk-in and backcountry sites.
fishing: Mazinaw Lake: lake trout, bass, walleye

trails.name: Cliff Top Trail
trails.detail: Ferry or paddle to Mazinaw Rock, then stairs to three lookout decks over the pictographs

trails.name: High Pines Trail
trails.detail: Loop through pine and hemlock forest and wetlands with a Mazinaw Lake glimpse

trails.name: Shield Trail
trails.detail: Canadian Shield loop on the old Addington Road through hardwoods and a beaver swamp

trails.name: Bon Echo Creek Trail
trails.detail: Short, leisurely walk along Bon Echo Creek (can be muddy)

trails.name: Abes and Essens Lake Trail
trails.detail: Backcountry loops (Clutes, Essens, Abes) with five hike-in campsites

name: Bonnechere
blurb: An Ottawa Valley classic where the Little Bonnechere River meets Round Lake. Sandy beach, river paddling, and the Footprints in Time walks.
fishing: Round Lake: lake trout, bass, walleye

campgrounds.sub: Old white pines · 43 Electrical · Sites 37 and 39 barrier-free

trails.name: McNaughton Trail
trails.detail: Footprints in Time interpretive walk along the Little Bonnechere River

name: Boyne Valley
blurb: Rolling headwater hills of the Boyne River, with the Murphy's Pinnacle lookout and Bruce Trail links. Day use only.
fishing: Boyne River: brook trout

trails.name: Primrose Loop
trails.detail: Hills and valley to the Murphy's Pinnacle lookout

name: Brent
blurb: Down 40 km of gravel to Cedar Lake and the ghost town of Brent. Rustic sites, the old Brent store, and serious quiet.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

campgrounds.sub: Cedar Lake · Non-electric · The old Brent store

name: Bronte Creek
blurb: The most urban park in the system, in Oakville. 144 all-electric sites in four small loops, plus a Play Barn, historic farm, and trails. Day-use and campground have separate entrances; no beach.
fishing: Bronte Creek, smallmouth bass, panfish

trails.name: Gateway Trail
trails.detail: The campground trail, an easy loop close to your site

trails.name: Maiden's Blush Trail
trails.detail: Paved forest loop, wheelchair accessible (day-use area)

trails.name: Trillium Trail
trails.detail: Spring wildflowers and valley views (day-use area)

trails.name: Half Moon Valley Trail
trails.detail: Ravine loop down to Bronte Creek with a boardwalk and some hills

trails.name: Ravine Trail
trails.detail: Valley-top path to a scenic ravine lookout

name: Caliper Lake
blurb: A quiet little park under old-growth red and white pines near Nestor Falls, with a sandy beach and pelicans, cranes and eagles on the lake.
fishing: Caliper Lake: walleye, pike, crappie, smallmouth bass

campgrounds.sub: Many electrical · Under the old red pines

name: Canisbay
blurb: KM 23. A big hardwood campground on Canisbay Lake, dog-free and radio-free, with no motorboats allowed.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

trails.name: Peck Lake
trails.detail: Circles the shoreline of Peck Lake (km 19.2)

trails.name: Track and Tower
trails.detail: Lookout over Cache Lake and old railway history (km 25)

name: Charleston Lake
blurb: A clear Canadian Shield lake in eastern Ontario near Brockville, with three wooded campgrounds, boat-in interior sites, and a strong trail network. Sandstone Island and the 10 km Tallow Rock Bay loop are highlights.
fishing: Charleston Lake: smallmouth and largemouth bass, lake trout, pike

campgrounds.sub: Younger forest, Sunnier sites, Some electric
By the beach, Electric and non-electric, Barrier-free sites

trails.name: Quiddity Trail
trails.detail: Boardwalk over a wetland to a scenic Charleston Lake lookout

trails.name: Sandstone Island Trail
trails.detail: Shoreline loop to a rock shelter used over 1,200 years ago

trails.name: Hemlock Ridge Trail
trails.detail: Hemlock ridge, a beaver pond, and sandstone rock crevices

trails.name: Shoreline Centennial Trail
trails.detail: An easy interpretive walk along the lakeshore

trails.name: Tallow Rock Bay Trail
trails.detail: The park's longest loop across the Frontenac Arch to Slim Bay's floating bridge

trails.name: Blue Mountain Trail
trails.detail: A water-access climb to the highest peak in Leeds County

name: Chutes
blurb: Waterfalls and the Seven Sisters rapids on the Aux Sables River, the only park campground on Highway 17 between Sudbury and the Sault.
fishing: Aux Sables River: pike, walleye, smallmouth bass

campgrounds.id: Red Pine Road & Seven Sisters Circle
Log Drive Lane & Big Chute Crescent

trails.name: Twin Bridges Trail
trails.detail: Falls, the Seven Sisters rapids, and river lookouts; short out-and-back option to the bridges

name: Craigleith
blurb: A shale beach on Georgian Bay at the foot of Blue Mountain, paved with 450-million-year-old fossils. Famous sunsets, ski country all around.
fishing: Georgian Bay: rainbow trout, salmon, bass

campgrounds.sub: Non-electric · Tents · Dog beach at the west end
Radio-free · Mostly electric · Near the day use area

name: Darlington
blurb: A lakefront park on Lake Ontario. Three campgrounds, Lakeside (all electric), Cliffside, and Hilltop, with beaches, birding, and marsh trails. A rail line runs past the park.
fishing: Lake Ontario & McLaughlin Bay, salmon, trout, bass, pike

campgrounds.sub: All electric · Radio-free · By the beach

trails.name: Burk Trail
trails.detail: Fields, forest, and a pioneer cemetery to a Lake Ontario lookout

trails.name: McLaughlin Bay Trail
trails.detail: Marsh ecosystem with nesting waterfowl and herons

trails.name: Robinson Creek Trail
trails.detail: Forested ravine loop from the Visitor Centre

trails.name: Waterfront Trail
trails.detail: A section of the 350 km Lake Ontario Waterfront Trail

name: Devil's Glen
blurb: A lookout over the Mad River gorge on the Niagara Escarpment; the Bruce Trail drops through the glen. Day use only.
fishing: Mad River: brook trout

name: Driftwood
blurb: A quiet Ottawa River bay where driftwood washes ashore, with long sandy beaches and Laurentian views across to Quebec.
fishing: Ottawa River: walleye, pike, smallmouth bass

campgrounds.sub: Waterfront · Non-electric · Site 121 at the quiet end

trails.name: Oak Highland Trail
trails.detail: Esker ridges with Laurentian views across the Ottawa

trails.name: Chevrier Creek Trail
trails.detail: Forested loops along the creek

name: Earl Rowe
blurb: The closest big family park to the city, on Earl Rowe Lake and the Boyne River near Alliston. Watch rainbow trout climb the fish ladder in spring and fall.
fishing: Earl Rowe Lake and the Boyne River: rainbow trout, bass, panfish

campgrounds.sub: Westside · Tents to large RVs · Site 102 barrier-free

trails.name: Little Trail
trails.detail: Short loop near the day use area

trails.name: Fletcher's Mill Pond
trails.detail: Around the mill pond to the fish ladder and dam

trails.name: Resource Trail
trails.detail: Interpretive loop through managed forest

trails.name: Lookout Trail
trails.detail: Climbs to a view over the reservoir and countryside

trails.name: Rainbow Run
trails.detail: Multi-use loop around the whole park, bike-friendly

name: Emily
blurb: A Kawarthas family park on the Pigeon River. Warm-water swimming, easy paddling, and big grassy sites.
fishing: Pigeon River: walleye, muskie, bass, panfish

campgrounds.sub: Mixed electric · Near the beach and amphitheatre

trails.name: Marsh Boardwalk Trail
trails.detail: Boardwalk into the Pigeon River cattail marsh, great for birds and turtles

name: Esker Lakes
blurb: Twenty-nine glacial kettle lakes strung between sandy eskers in the boreal forest. Home of the August Blueberry Festival.
fishing: Panagapka and area lakes: brook trout, lake trout, pike, perch

campgrounds.sub: Panagapka Lake · Over half electrical · Dog beach

trails.name: Lonesome Bog Trail
trails.detail: Boardwalk loop around a black spruce bog

trails.name: Trapper's Trail
trails.detail: To Panagapka and Lallan Lakes, the backcountry access

name: Fairbank
blurb: A crystal-clear, spring-fed lake on the rim of the Sudbury Basin meteorite crater. Waterfront sites you can launch a canoe from.
fishing: Fairbank Lake: smallmouth bass, lake trout

trails.name: Wa-Shai-Ga-Mog Trail
trails.detail: Bedrock climb to views of Fairbank Lake and the Fairbank Fault

name: Ferris
blurb: Ranney Falls and a 300 foot suspension bridge over the Trent River gorge, with drumlin-top campsites minutes from Campbellford.
fishing: Trent River: bass, walleye, pike

campgrounds.sub: Drumlin top · Valley views in the 40s · Showers
Larger · Some electrical · Near the playground

trails.name: Ranney Falls Trail
trails.detail: The old park road to the falls and the suspension bridge over the gorge

trails.name: Drumlin Trail System
trails.detail: Three linked loops (Blue, White, Red) over forested drumlins

name: Finlayson Point
blurb: A peninsula in island-dotted Lake Temagami, the only campground on the lake. Some sites come with their own docks.
fishing: Lake Temagami: lake trout, walleye, bass, pike, whitefish

trails.name: Caribou Mountain Lookout
trails.detail: Up to the fire tower over the town and Lake Temagami

name: Fitzroy
blurb: Century-old white pines where the Carp River meets the Ottawa. Two beaches, big riverside sites, 40 minutes from downtown Ottawa.
fishing: Ottawa and Carp Rivers: walleye, bass, pike, muskie

campgrounds.sub: Largest · Mostly electrical · Near the store
Near the beaches · Some Ottawa River views

trails.name: Terraces Trail
trails.detail: Glacial terraces above the Carp River

trails.name: Carp Trail
trails.detail: Riverside walk under 300 year old bur oaks

name: Forks of the Credit
blurb: The Credit River splits through a gorge past the Cataract falls and old mill ruins. Kettle lakes and bare rolling hills. Day use only.
fishing: Credit River: brook and brown trout (special regulations)

trails.name: Kettle Trail
trails.detail: Around a glacial kettle lake

trails.name: Meadow Trail
trails.detail: Open rolling hills above the gorge

trails.name: Trans Canada Trail
trails.detail: Along the gorge to the Cataract falls and ruins

name: French River
blurb: Backcountry camping only along Canada's first Heritage River, 105 km from Lake Nipissing to Georgian Bay. 291 numbered sites on the shorelines, all water access, permits from the Visitor Centre.
fishing: French River: walleye, pike, muskie, bass

trails.name: Recollet Falls Trail
trails.detail: From the Visitor Centre along the gorge to the falls

name: Frontenac
blurb: Backcountry camping only, 20 minutes north of Kingston: 54 numbered sites in 13 clusters and 6 singles, every one reachable by both canoe and trail. 22 lakes, 100 km of loops, open all four seasons.
fishing: 22 lakes: lake trout, bass, pike (varies by lake)

trails.name: Arab Lake Gorge Trail
trails.detail: Boardwalk through a mossy gorge by the park office

trails.name: Doe Lake Trail
trails.detail: The classic intro loop past Doe Lake

trails.name: Big Salmon Lake Loop
trails.detail: Full-day circuit of the big lake

trails.name: Slide Lake Loop
trails.detail: The park's toughest loop, cliffs over Slide Lake

name: Fushimi Lake
blurb: A pristine boreal lake northwest of Hearst with secluded, all-electrical sites, a historic fire tower to hike to, and paddle-in backcountry beyond.
fishing: Fushimi Lake: walleye, northern pike

trails.name: Fire Tower Trail
trails.detail: To the 1930s Bannerman fire tower

name: Grundy Lake
blurb: Nine campgrounds across four inland lakes. Smooth-rock swimming, quiet water, no motorboats.
fishing: Northern pike, bass, panfish

trails.name: Swan Lake
trails.detail: Nature reserve; rocky ridges, lookouts, and a wetland boardwalk

trails.name: Gut Lake
trails.detail: Most popular; Canadian Shield rock, lakes and streams, a rope swing

trails.name: Beaver Dams
trails.detail: Forest and wetlands; a Great Blue Heron rookery and a beaver dam

trails.name: Pakeshkag Lake
trails.detail: Multi-use linear trail past Beaver Lake wetland and backcountry sites

name: Halfway Lake
blurb: Rugged Shield country on the edge of the boreal forest, with two campgrounds on Raven Lake and moose along the Echo Pond Trail.
fishing: Halfway and Raven Lakes: walleye, pike, lake trout

campgrounds.sub: All electrical · Near the store and main beach

trails.name: Echo Pond Trail
trails.detail: Pond loop where moose are often spotted

trails.name: Osprey Heights Trail
trails.detail: Steady climb to cliff tops over Halfway Lake

trails.name: Hawk Ridge Trail
trails.detail: Backcountry loop with five hike-in campsites

name: Inverhuron
blurb: Lake Huron beach, dunes, and rocky shallows near Tiverton. One of the hardest parks in Ontario to book, and worth it.
fishing: Lake Huron: salmon, rainbow trout, bass, perch

name: Ivanhoe Lake
blurb: A warm, shallow boreal lake with a big wading beach near Foleyet, ringed by four waterfront campgrounds and quaking kettle bogs.
fishing: Ivanhoe Lake: walleye, pike, perch

campgrounds.sub: Mostly electrical · Barrier-free 15 and 16 · Red Pine beach
Non-electric · Comfort station · Up the hill
Non-electric · Comfort station · Up the hill

name: John E. Pearce
blurb: Old-growth Carolinian forest on top of the Lake Erie bluffs, with a museum in the old Backus-Page house next door. Day use only.
fishing: Lake Erie below the bluffs: perch, bass

trails.name: Spicebush Trail
trails.detail: Carolinian loop above the Erie bluffs

name: Kakabeka Falls
blurb: The Niagara of the North: a 40 metre curtain on the Kaministiquia River with boardwalk platforms on both rims, half an hour from Thunder Bay.
fishing: Kaministiquia River: walleye, pike, bass

campgrounds.sub: Mostly electrical · Site 76 pull-through · Laundry · Up the big hill

trails.name: Boardwalk
trails.detail: Platforms on both rims of the falls

trails.name: Mountain Portage Trail
trails.detail: The voyageurs' portage around the falls

trails.name: Little Falls Trail
trails.detail: Down to a smaller cascade on the river

trails.name: Beaver Meadows Trail
trails.detail: Meadow and forest loop above the gorge

name: Kawartha Highlands
blurb: Backcountry camping only: over 100 numbered paddle-in sites across six canoe loops between cottage country and the Shield. Reserve the exact site, then earn it with a portage.
fishing: 300 lakes: lake trout, brook trout, walleye, bass, panfish

name: Kearney Lake
blurb: KM 36.5. Mid-size and non-electric, with two beaches on Kearney Lake.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

name: Kettle Lakes
blurb: Twenty-two spring-fed glacial kettle lakes in the jack pine east of Timmins, with a 14 km bike trail system threading between them.
fishing: Stocked kettle lakes: rainbow, brook and lake trout

campgrounds.sub: Two thirds electrical · Comfort station · Site 97 very private

name: Killarney
blurb: A wilderness park of pink granite and white La Cloche quartzite ridges that inspired the Group of Seven. One car campground on George Lake, plus a legendary canoe interior and the 78 km La Cloche Silhouette Trail.
fishing: George Lake and Georgian Bay: smallmouth bass, pike

trails.name: Granite Ridge Trail
trails.detail: Climbs to two lookouts over the La Cloche Mountains and Georgian Bay

trails.name: Cranberry Bog Trail
trails.detail: Bogs and marshes to A.Y. Jackson Lake, rich in birds and wildlife

trails.name: Chikanishing Trail
trails.detail: Windswept Georgian Bay shoreline with logging-era iron rings

trails.name: Lake of the Woods Trail
trails.detail: Old-growth forest and rocky outcrops with Silver Peak views

trails.name: The Crack
trails.detail: A steep boulder scramble to a panoramic La Cloche ridge lookout

trails.name: La Cloche Silhouette Trail
trails.detail: The legendary multi-day backcountry loop from George Lake

name: Killbear
blurb: Seven campgrounds on a Georgian Bay peninsula, windswept pines, big sand beaches. No backcountry.
fishing: Smallmouth bass, lake trout, walleye, pike, perch

trails.name: Lighthouse Point
trails.detail: Short loop to the tip of Killbear Point with Georgian Bay views

trails.name: Twin Points
trails.detail: Rock outcrops and forest out to rocky points and sandy beaches

trails.name: Lookout Point
trails.detail: Forest and rock to a panoramic Georgian Bay lookout at the midpoint

trails.name: Recreational Trail
trails.detail: Runs the length of the park; popular for hiking and biking

name: Kiosk
blurb: Algonquin's remote northwest corner on Kioshkokwi Lake. A small, simple campground and a canoe-trip launch pad.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

name: Komoka
blurb: Thames River valley forest on London's doorstep, laced with walking and biking trails. Day use only.
fishing: Thames River: smallmouth bass, pike, walleye

name: Lake St. Peter
blurb: A small, quiet lake park just east of Algonquin near Maynooth. Spacious forest sites, a little beach, and loons at night.
fishing: Lake St. Peter: lake trout, bass

name: Lake Superior
blurb: A vast wilderness park on Superior's east shore. The Agawa Rock pictographs, Old Woman Bay, and some of the best day hiking in Ontario.
fishing: Lake trout, brook trout, salmon; brook trout in the inland lakes

trails.name: Agawa Rock Pictographs
trails.detail: Rock ledge on Superior where generations of Ojibwe recorded dreams in red ochre

trails.name: Trapper's Trail
trails.detail: Boardwalk loop around a beaver pond, good odds of moose

trails.name: South Old Woman River
trails.detail: Criss-crosses the river from Rabbit Blanket Lake Campground

trails.name: Nokomis
trails.detail: Lookouts over Old Woman Bay; ranked a top-five Canadian day hike

trails.name: Pinguisibi
trails.detail: Follows the Sand River past falls and rapids

trails.name: Orphan Lake
trails.detail: Loop to a Superior pebble beach and the Baldhead River falls

trails.name: Awausee
trails.detail: Big lookout loop over the Agawa Valley

trails.name: Peat Mountain
trails.detail: Climbs to a panorama above Rabbit Blanket Lake

trails.name: Coastal Trail
trails.detail: The legendary multi-day shoreline scramble from Agawa to Chalfant Cove

name: Lake of Two Rivers
blurb: KM 31. The most scenic corridor campground, next to the Visitor Centre on Lake of Two Rivers.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

trails.name: Two Rivers
trails.detail: Short climb to a cliff-top lookout by the Visitor Centre (km 31)

name: Long Point
blurb: A Lake Erie sandspit and UNESCO biosphere near Port Rowan, famous for birding and 2 km of soft sand beach. Four campgrounds, some right in the dunes. Beach-focused, with little hiking.
fishing: Long Point Bay and Lake Erie: bass, walleye, perch, pike

campgrounds.sub: Electric and non-electric, Right behind the dunes
Electric and non-electric, Right behind the dunes

name: MacGregor Point
blurb: A Lake Huron park near Port Elgin known for the Huron Fringe Birding Festival, with three campgrounds, coastal wetlands, and sunset beaches. Trails and boardwalks wind through fen and forest.
fishing: Lake Huron: bass, pike, salmon, trout

trails.name: Huron Fringe Trail
trails.detail: Barrier-free boardwalk loop by the Visitor Centre through fen, swamp, and shore

trails.name: Tower Trail
trails.detail: Boardwalk and towers over marsh, good for herons, bitterns, and turtles

trails.name: Old Shore Road Trail
trails.detail: Follows the original shore road under maples and oaks along Lake Huron

trails.name: Deer Run Trail
trails.detail: Multi-use loop linking the three campgrounds and the Park Store

trails.name: Lake Ridge Trail
trails.detail: A wooded loop through the heart of the Carolinian forest

name: MacLeod
blurb: Named for an abandoned gold mine, wrapped around a lagoon on Kenogamisis Lake off Highway 11. Treed sites, many with their own shoreline.
fishing: Kenogamisis Lake: walleye, pike, perch

trails.name: MacLeod Trail
trails.detail: Nature loop near the group camp and comfort station

name: Makobe-Grays River
blurb: A spring whitewater run from the Ishpatina highlands down to Elk Lake, threading old burns and esker country. Backcountry permits only.
fishing: Makobe River: brook trout, walleye

name: Mara
blurb: A small beach park on Lake Simcoe minutes from Orillia. One of the lake's best sandy beaches, with warm, shallow swimming.
fishing: Lake Simcoe: perch, bass, walleye, lake trout

campgrounds.sub: Electric and non-electric · Steps from the beach

trails.name: Riverside Trail
trails.detail: Short creekside nature loop with herons and osprey

name: Mark S. Burnham
blurb: One of the last old-growth stands in the Kawarthas, minutes east of Peterborough. Giant maples, hemlocks and a quiet loop trail. Day use only.
fishing: No notable fishery

name: Marten River
blurb: The southern gateway to Temagami, with old-growth white pines, a recreated logging camp, and riverside sites on the Marten River.
fishing: Marten River system: walleye, pike, smallmouth bass

trails.name: Old Growth Trail
trails.detail: Immense old-growth white pines, roots and rock (red loop)

name: McRae Point
blurb: Big pull-through sites on a Lake Simcoe point near Orillia, with boat launch access to the Trent-Severn Waterway.
fishing: Lake Simcoe: perch, bass, pike, lake trout

campgrounds.sub: All electrical pull-throughs · Sites 51 and 53 barrier-free
Two loops · Electric and non-electric · More private

trails.name: Water's Edge Trail
trails.detail: Lake Simcoe shoreline and a hardwood-swamp boardwalk from the boat launch

name: Mew Lake
blurb: KM 30.6. Open year-round with yurts, electrical sites, and the Old Railway Bike Trail.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

name: Mikisew
blurb: A quiet Almaguin park on Eagle Lake with three sandy beaches, island paddling, disc golf, and a great dog beach.
fishing: Eagle Lake: bass, walleye, pike, panfish

trails.name: The Point Trail
trails.detail: A narrow point jutting into Eagle Lake, perfect picnic spot

trails.name: Narrows Trail
trails.detail: Walk or bike from the campground to the Eagle Lake Narrows country store

name: Misery Bay
blurb: Manitoulin Island's quiet corner: rare coastal alvars, flat limestone shores, and 15 km of trails to Lake Huron. Day use only.
fishing: Lake Huron shallows: bass, pike

name: Missinaibi
blurb: Barclay Bay on Missinaibi Lake is the trailhead of a legend: 500 km of Heritage River running all the way to James Bay, in the world's largest game preserve.
fishing: Missinaibi Lake: lake trout, walleye, northern pike

campgrounds.sub: Non-electric · Food lockers · Big dock system

trails.name: Borasso Logging Camp Trail
trails.detail: Remnants of the 1950s logging camp on Baltic Bay

name: Mississagi
blurb: Ancient Penokean Hills north of Elliot Lake, run in partnership with Serpent River First Nation. Secluded Semiwite Lake sites and 40 km of ridge trails.
fishing: Semiwite, Flack and Helenbar Lakes: brook trout, lake trout, bass

campgrounds.sub: Secluded pull-throughs · North shore of Semiwite Lake

trails.name: Semiwite Creek Trail
trails.detail: Creekside loop from the campground

trails.name: Helenbar Lookout Trail
trails.detail: Ridge climb to the lookout over Helenbar Lake

trails.name: Cobre Lake Trail
trails.detail: Full-day loop through the Penokean Hills

name: Mono Cliffs
blurb: Crevice caves, cliff-top lookouts, and a jade-green kettle lake on the Niagara Escarpment. Bruce Trail country. Day use only.
fishing: McCarston's Lake is protected, no angling

trails.name: Carriage Trail
trails.detail: The old carriage road, the spine of the park

trails.name: Cliff-Top Side Trail
trails.detail: Metal stairways to lookouts over the crevice caves

trails.name: Spillway Trail
trails.detail: Along a glacial spillway valley

trails.name: McCarston's Lake Trail
trails.detail: Loops the jade kettle lake

name: Murphys Point
blurb: A Big Rideau Lake park on the Rideau Waterway, home to the historic Silver Queen mica mine. Hardwood campgrounds, sandy Hogg Bay, lakeside trails.
fishing: Big Rideau Lake: bass, lake trout, pike

campgrounds.sub: Electrical pull-throughs · Ash Hill loop · Near the beach
Non-electric loops · Tents · Central comfort station

trails.name: Point Trail
trails.detail: To the tip of the peninsula on Big Rideau Lake

trails.name: Sylvan Trail
trails.detail: Forest loop linking McParlan House and the Point Trail

trails.name: Lally Homestead Trail
trails.detail: Old farm fields and wildflowers

trails.name: Silver Queen Mine Trail
trails.detail: To the historic mica mine, guided tours in summer

name: Nagagamisis
blurb: 'Lake of fine, white sand' in Ojibwe. Spruce and jack pine moraines on the edge of the Clay Belt, north of Hornepayne on Highway 631.
fishing: Nagagamisis Lake: walleye, pike, perch

trails.name: Time Trail
trails.detail: Interpretive loop through the boreal forest

name: Neys
blurb: A driftwood-strewn Superior beach park near Marathon, once the wartime Neys 100 prisoner-of-war camp. Pic Island views the Group of Seven painted.
fishing: Lake Superior and the Little Pic River: lake trout, salmon, pike

trails.name: Point Trail
trails.detail: Shoreline walk from Prisoners Cove to Prisoners Point

trails.name: Under the Volcano
trails.detail: Volcanic rock formations beyond Prisoners Point

trails.name: Dune Trail
trails.detail: Loop through the fragile dune ecosystem behind the beach

trails.name: Lookout Trail
trails.detail: Climbs above Area 4 for views over Ashburton Bay

trails.name: Kopa Cove Trail
trails.detail: Linear route to a rugged cobble cove

trails.name: Pic Island Overlook
trails.detail: To the view of Pic Island that Lawren Harris painted

name: North Beach
blurb: A sandy barrier beach between North Bay lagoon and Lake Ontario in Prince Edward County. Sandbanks' quiet little sibling. Day use only.
fishing: North Bay lagoon: bass, panfish

name: Oastler Lake
blurb: A small cottage-country lake five minutes from Parry Sound, with waterfront campsites, polished-rock shoreline, and famous sunsets.
fishing: Oastler Lake: bass, pike, rainbow trout

campgrounds.sub: All electrical · Pull-throughs · By the beach

name: Obabika River
blurb: Home of the old-growth red and white pines of the Obabika forest and the spirit rocks of Chee-skon Lake. Backcountry permits only.
fishing: Obabika and area lakes: lake trout, walleye, bass

name: Ojibway
blurb: Tranquil and wild on Little Vermilion Lake near Sioux Lookout. A sandy beach, pine forest walks, and muskie in the weed lines.
fishing: Little Vermilion Lake: muskie, walleye, pike, smallmouth bass

name: Ouimet Canyon
blurb: A sheer-sided gorge 100 metres deep and 150 wide, with arctic plants surviving on the shadowed floor. Boardwalk to two rim platforms. Day use only.
fishing: No fishing in the canyon

trails.name: Canyon Trail
trails.detail: Boardwalk loop to two platforms over the 100 m deep canyon

name: Oxtongue River, Ragged Falls
blurb: A short walk to Ragged Falls, one of cottage country's biggest cascades, as the Oxtongue pours out of Algonquin. Day use only.
fishing: Oxtongue River: brook trout, bass

trails.name: Ragged Falls Trail
trails.detail: Up the riverside to the thundering falls

name: Pakwash
blurb: Secluded and serene on the Red Lake road, with a long sandy beach and shallow warm water. The last park before the road runs out.
fishing: Pakwash Lake: walleye, pike, smallmouth bass, lake trout

name: Pancake Bay
blurb: Three kilometres of fine white sand on Lake Superior, shallow water that actually warms up, and the lookout over the bay where the Edmund Fitzgerald sank.
fishing: Lake Superior: lake trout, salmon, whitefish

campgrounds.sub: Sites 1-25 radio and generator-free · Forested hill · Large sites
Closest to the gatehouse · Short paths to the beach

Near the group sites and the Nature Trail

trails.detail: Forest and fen loop behind the campground

trails.name: Lookout Trail
trails.detail: Up to the lookout over the bay and the Edmund Fitzgerald's crossing

name: Petroglyphs
blurb: The Teaching Rocks: Canada's largest concentration of Indigenous rock carvings, protected at the Learning Place. Day use only.
fishing: McGinnis Lake is protected, no angling

trails.name: Nanabush Trail
trails.detail: Forest loop past wetlands and ridges near the Teaching Rocks

name: Pigeon River
blurb: Waterfalls and gorge hikes on the river that marks the Canada-US border, at the very start of Ontario. Day use only.
fishing: Pigeon River: pike, walleye, smallmouth bass

trails.name: High Falls Trail
trails.detail: To the 28 m falls on the border river

trails.name: Finger Point Trail
trails.detail: Climb to a lookout over Lake Superior

name: Pinery
blurb: A rare oak savanna on Lake Huron with 10 km of sand beach and the paddleable Old Ausable Channel. Very busy in summer.
fishing: Old Ausable Channel: largemouth bass, pike, panfish

trails.name: Cedar Trail
trails.detail: Rare Oak Savanna with a viewing platform and a branch to the Lake Huron beach

trails.name: Riverside Trail
trails.detail: Old Ausable Channel floodplain; wheelchair accessible with viewing platforms

trails.name: Heritage Trail
trails.detail: Diverse habitats out to an Old Ausable Channel overlook

trails.name: Wilderness Trail
trails.detail: Loops to a sandy beach lookout on Lake Huron

trails.name: Carolinian Trail
trails.detail: Hilly floodplain forest of rare Carolinian species

trails.name: Nipissing Trail
trails.detail: Climbs Pinery's oldest, largest dune ridge for a park-wide view

name: Pog Lake
blurb: KM 37. The largest corridor campground, with electrical sites and the quiet Section C.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

trails.name: Centennial Ridges
trails.detail: Dramatic ridge-top lookouts; a full-day hike (km 37.6)

name: Point Farms
blurb: On a 20 metre bluff over Lake Huron north of Goderich, where a grand Victorian resort once stood. Legendary sunsets and a great dog beach.
fishing: Lake Huron: salmon, trout, bass, perch

campgrounds.sub: All equipment · Pull-throughs · Site 55 lake-view electric
All equipment · Pull-throughs · Site 55 lake-view electric

trails.name: The Old Farms Trail
trails.detail: Through the fields and orchards of the old Victorian resort

name: Port Bruce
blurb: A sandy Lake Erie swimming beach at the mouth of Catfish Creek, south of Aylmer. Day use only.
fishing: Catfish Creek and Lake Erie: perch, bass, rainbow runs

name: Port Burwell
blurb: A huge warm Lake Erie beach below the bluffs, plus a historic lighthouse and the HMCS Ojibwa submarine in the village.
fishing: Lake Erie and Big Otter Creek: perch, bass, walleye

campgrounds.sub: Sunny and grassy · Near the play field

trails.name: Point Loop
trails.detail: Forested loop from the play field trailheads

trails.name: Beach Trail
trails.detail: Along the Lake Erie shore below the bluffs

name: Potholes
blurb: Glacial meltwater drilled smooth cylinders into the bedrock of the Kinniwabi River east of Wawa. A short walk off Highway 101. Day use only.
fishing: Kinniwabi River: brook trout

trails.name: Potholes Trail
trails.detail: Short walk to the glacial potholes on the Kinniwabi

name: Presqu'ile
blurb: A Lake Ontario peninsula famous for spring and fall bird migration and monarch butterflies. Eight campgrounds, a long sandy beach, a marsh boardwalk, and a historic lighthouse.
fishing: Presqu'ile Bay and Lake Ontario: bass, pike, walleye, panfish

campgrounds.sub: Mostly electric, Open and sunny, Best waterfront
Non-electric, A few on the lakeshore
Mostly electric, Near the rocky shore
Mostly non-electric, 6 Walk-in on Lake Ontario

trails.name: Marsh Boardwalk
trails.detail: Barrier-free boardwalk into the cattail marsh with two viewing towers

trails.name: Jobes' Woods Trail
trails.detail: Old maples, vernal pools, and boardwalks through an old-growth woodlot

trails.name: Owen Point Trail
trails.detail: Five lookouts over the natural beach and island bird colonies

trails.name: Pioneer Trail
trails.detail: Forest, plantations, and old fields in the heart of the peninsula

trails.name: Newcastle Trail
trails.detail: The park's longest loop through mature beech-maple forest

name: Queen Elizabeth II Wildlands
blurb: Ontario's best kept secret: 33,000 hectares of crown wilderness with no gates, no fees, and no facilities, hiding in plain sight south of Algonquin. Not reservable, not operating, not on the map. Yet here you are.
fishing: Dozens of unnamed lakes: bass, pike, the occasional rumour of trout

trails.name: Ganaraska Trail, Wilderness Section
trails.detail: Unmarked crown wilderness crossing for experienced navigators only

name: Quetico
blurb: The legendary canoe wilderness. Dawson Trail on French Lake is its only drive-in campground, two loops at the threshold of a million acres of paddling.
fishing: French Lake and beyond: walleye, pike, lake trout, bass (barbless, artificial only)

trails.name: Pines Trail
trails.detail: From Dawson Trail to the beaches on Pickerel Lake

name: Raccoon Lake
blurb: KM 41. The smallest and quietest campground on the corridor.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

trails.name: Spruce Bog Boardwalk
trails.detail: Accessible boardwalk across two black spruce bogs; great birding (km 42.5)

trails.name: Beaver Pond
trails.detail: Two beaver ponds and rocky lookouts (km 45.2)

name: Rainbow Falls
blurb: Two campgrounds five kilometres apart: Whitesand Lake with its cascading falls, and Rossport on the bare Superior shore by the fishing village.
fishing: Whitesand Lake and Superior: pike, walleye, trout, salmon

campgrounds.sub: On Lake Superior · 23 Electrical · Open birch grove
Loops Maggie, Selim, Whitesand, Lakeside · Lakeside up on the hill

trails.detail: Through the old campground to a Superior panorama

trails.name: Casque Isles Trail
trails.detail: Coastal through-trail linking Rossport, Schreiber and Terrace Bay

name: Rene Brunelle
blurb: Remi Lake was a 1920s floatplane base, and the bush pilot story lives on in this quiet park near Kapuskasing. Four sandy beaches and secluded, wooded sites.
fishing: Remi Lake: walleye, pike, smallmouth bass

campgrounds.id: Walk-In Sites, Electrical Sites & Camp Cabin

name: Restoule
blurb: A quiet, off-the-beaten-path park near North Bay with three lakeside campgrounds, big fishing lakes, and the must-do Fire Tower Trail up Stormy Lake Bluff.
fishing: Restoule and Stormy Lakes: walleye, lake trout, pike, muskie, smallmouth bass

campgrounds.sub: Non-electric, Tents and small RVs, 10 Walk-in, Dog beach
All electric, Near the main beach and trails

trails.detail: Boardwalks and rocky ridges to a historic fire tower and the 100 m Stormy Lake Bluff view

trails.name: Angel's Point Trail
trails.detail: Hardwood-forest loops shared with mountain bikers

name: Rideau River
blurb: Level, shaded campsites on the banks of the Rideau Canal system, 40 minutes from Ottawa. Herons, turtles, and slow-river paddling.
fishing: Rideau River: muskie, bass, walleye, panfish

trails.name: Shoreline Trail
trails.detail: Riverside connector between the beaches and campgrounds, good birding

name: Rock Lake
blurb: KM 40, down Rock Lake Road. Pictographs, canoe access, and a busy beach.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

trails.name: Lookout Trail
trails.detail: Steep climb to a famous panoramic lookout (km 39.7)

trails.name: Booth's Rock
trails.detail: Two lakes and a cliff lookout, returning on an old railbed (Rock Lake Rd)

name: Rock Point
blurb: A 350-million-year-old coral reef on Lake Erie. Fossils in the limestone shelf, a shell-strewn beach, and monarch butterflies each September.
fishing: Lake Erie and the Grand River mouth: bass, walleye, perch, catfish

trails.name: Woodlot Trail
trails.detail: Carolinian forest and oak savanna loop

trails.name: Lookout Trail
trails.detail: Shore route to the dune viewing platform, lots of stairs

name: Rondeau
blurb: A Carolinian sandspit between Lake Erie and Rondeau Bay. Old-growth tulip trees, 11 km of beach, and warbler-famous birding.
fishing: Rondeau Bay: largemouth bass, perch, crappie, pike

trails.name: Warbler's Way
trails.detail: The park's shortest loop, big during spring migration

trails.name: Tulip Tree Trail
trails.detail: Accessible boardwalk through mature Carolinian forest, Prothonotary Warbler territory

trails.name: Black Oak Trail
trails.detail: Pine-oak savanna with Wood Lily and Woodland Sunflower meadows

trails.name: Spicebush Trail
trails.detail: Old-growth tulip trees, beech, and a spring wildflower carpet

trails.name: Marsh Trail
trails.detail: One-way along Rondeau Bay through the big wetland; bring bug spray

trails.name: South Point Trail
trails.detail: Forest walk to the quiet south tip of the peninsula

trails.name: Harrison Trail
trails.detail: The multi-use spine of the park through oak woodland; bike-friendly

name: Rushing River
blurb: Rapids tumble past the campground into Dogtooth Lake, 20 minutes from Kenora. The northwest's favourite family park since 1958.
fishing: Dogtooth Lake: walleye, pike, smallmouth bass, lake trout

name: Samuel de Champlain
blurb: Voyageur country on the Mattawa River, with the fur-trade heritage centre, Moore Lake beaches, and the rugged Etienne trails.
fishing: Moore Lake and the Mattawa River: bass, pike, walleye

campgrounds.sub: All electrical · Campers beach · Near the Visitor Centre

trails.detail: Marsh boardwalk on a peninsula, lots of wildlife

name: Sandbanks
blurb: The world's largest baymouth sandbar, with dune beaches on Lake Ontario and calm swimming on West Lake. Books out fast.
fishing: West and East Lake: bass, pike, panfish

trails.detail: Quick link across fields and forest between the park's two sides

name: Sandbar Lake
blurb: A sandy esker lake fifteen minutes north of Ignace, the jumping-off point for wilderness canoe routes deeper into the northwest.
fishing: Sandbar Lake: walleye, pike, perch

campgrounds.sub: Mixed electric · Near the esker beach

name: Sauble Falls
blurb: A small riverside park where the Sauble tumbles over its falls. Watch trout and salmon leap upstream, minutes from Sauble Beach.
fishing: Sauble River: rainbow trout and salmon runs; Lake Huron nearby

trails.name: Sauble Falls Trail
trails.detail: Forest and ancient dune loop from the East Campground

name: Selkirk
blurb: A quiet, grassy Lake Erie park where Sandusk Creek meets the lake. Great paddling, catfishing, and spring bird migration.
fishing: Sandusk Creek and Lake Erie: catfish, bass, perch

trails.name: Wheeler's Walk
trails.detail: Boardwalk along the Sandusk Creek marsh and a white pine plantation

name: Sharbot Lake
blurb: Two clear lakes off Highway 7. Most of the park hugs sheltered Black Lake, with a short portage over to big Sharbot Lake.
fishing: Sharbot and Black Lakes: bass, walleye, pike, lake trout

campgrounds.sub: Mature trees · 15 Electrical · Comfort station and laundry

name: Short Hills
blurb: Niagara's carved valleys and Swayze Falls, with meadow and forest loops shared with horses and bikes. Day use only.
fishing: Twelve Mile Creek: brook trout (catch and release)

trails.name: Swayze Falls Trail
trails.detail: Wide meadow-and-forest loop to the falls platform

trails.name: Terrace Creek Trail
trails.detail: Valley crossing in the heart of the hills

name: Sibbald Point
blurb: One of Ontario's busiest parks, a big sandy Lake Simcoe beach, with 600+ shaded sites split between the non-electric East and all-electric West campgrounds.
fishing: Lake Simcoe, perch, bass, pike, lake trout

trails.name: Maidenhair Fern Trail
trails.detail: Forest and varied ecosystems on the park's quiet south side

trails.name: Cultural History Trail
trails.detail: Short walk past historical features including Eildon Hall

name: Silent Lake
blurb: A quiet, motor-free lake near Bancroft with two forested campgrounds and plenty of walk-in sites. Good for paddling and fishing, with hiking and mountain-bike trails.
fishing: Silent Lake: smallmouth and largemouth bass, lake trout

campgrounds.sub: Walk-in loops, Near the beach, Yurts

trails.detail: A long loop encircling Silent Lake

name: Silver Falls
blurb: Rustic camping on Dog Lake north of Thunder Bay, with the classic 10 km trail along the Kaministiquia to Silver and Dog Falls.
fishing: Dog Lake: walleye, pike, smallmouth bass, whitefish

trails.name: Silver Falls Trail
trails.detail: Along the Kaministiquia to Silver and Dog Falls, lookout at the summit

name: Silver Lake
blurb: A small clear-lake park on Highway 7 with cliff-top tent sites and two sandy beaches. The quieter neighbour to Sharbot Lake.
fishing: Silver Lake: pike, smallmouth bass

campgrounds.sub: Mixed electric · Near the small beach and comfort station

name: Sioux Narrows
blurb: Bedrock, towering white pines, and pelicans on Lake of the Woods. A small park on a legendary walleye lake with centuries-old pictographs a boat ride away.
fishing: Lake of the Woods: walleye, pike, smallmouth bass, muskie

campgrounds.sub: One third electrical · Eight walk-in waterfront sites · Bedrock and pine

name: Six Mile Lake
blurb: A small, family-favourite Muskoka park just off Highway 400 with seven campgrounds, three beaches, and calm, sheltered water for paddling. One trail honours painter David Milne.
fishing: Six Mile Lake: bass, pike, panfish

name: Sleeping Giant
blurb: The Giant sleeps across Thunder Bay, and the Marie Louise Lake campground sits at his feet. Home of the hardest and most rewarding hikes in any Ontario park.
fishing: Marie Louise Lake and Superior: pike, walleye, lake trout

trails.name: Sea Lion Trail
trails.detail: Short walk to the famous rock arch

trails.name: Middlebrun Bay Trail

trails.detail: To the knees and the highest cliff lookout in Ontario

trails.name: Kabeyun Trail
trails.detail: The shoreline spine to the Giant's feet

name: Solace
blurb: A chain of quiet lakes on the western edge of the Temagami wilderness, linked by rugged portages. Backcountry permits only.
fishing: Lake trout, walleye, pike

name: Spanish River
blurb: The classic whitewater canoe trip: pool-and-drop rapids down the Spanish to the Elbow, with rail access to the put-ins. Backcountry permits only.
fishing: Spanish River: walleye, pike, smallmouth bass

name: Springwater
blurb: Pine plantations, ponds, and picnic lawns just outside Barrie. Quiet, easy walking under tall trees. Day use only.
fishing: No notable fishery

name: Sturgeon Bay
region: Near North Park · Pointe au Baril
blurb: A tiny Georgian Bay park in the 30,000 Islands. Sites 6 to 15 look straight down Sturgeon Bay, and the fishing is serious.
fishing: Sturgeon Bay and Georgian Bay: walleye, pike, muskie, bass

name: Sturgeon River
blurb: A wild river corridor pouring out of Temagami country toward Lake Nipissing, with whitewater for experienced trippers. Backcountry permits only.
fishing: Sturgeon River: walleye, pike, sturgeon (protected)

name: Tea Lake
blurb: KM 11 on the Highway 60 corridor. A small, simple campground where you can paddle straight out onto Canoe Lake.
fishing: Brook trout, lake trout, smallmouth bass (special fishery)

trails.name: Whiskey Rapids
trails.detail: Follows the Oxtongue River to Whiskey Rapids (km 7.2)

trails.name: Hardwood Lookout
trails.detail: Hardwood forest to a view over Smoke Lake (km 13.8)

trails.name: Mizzy Lake
trails.detail: Best trail for wildlife; nine ponds; no dogs allowed (km 15.4)

name: The Massasauga
blurb: Backcountry camping only among hundreds of windswept Georgian Bay islands from Parry Sound to the Moon River. 135 numbered water-access sites, bayside or inland lakes, in rattlesnake country.
fishing: Georgian Bay and inland lakes: bass, pike, walleye, lake trout

trails.name: Wreck Island Trail
trails.detail: Boat-access loop over billion year old twisted rock

name: Tidewater
blurb: Five islands in the Moose River estuary, 20 km from James Bay. Water taxi from Moosonee, seals and belugas offshore. Operated with Moose Cree First Nation.
fishing: Moose River estuary: pike, walleye, brook trout, whitefish

name: Turkey Point
blurb: A bluff-top park over Long Point Bay with a 2 km shallow beach and the only golf course in an Ontario park.
fishing: Long Point Bay: perch, bass, walleye, pike

trails.name: Oak Savanna Trail
trails.detail: Loop through rare oak savanna habitat

trails.name: Lookout Bluff Trail
trails.detail: Bluff-top views over Long Point Bay, starts near the park office

trails.name: Fin and Feather Trail
trails.detail: Past the old hatchery pond to the Normandale Fish Hatchery

name: Voyageur
blurb: Four beaches on the wide Ottawa River at the Quebec border, named for the fur-trade canoes that once portaged these rapids.
fishing: Ottawa River: walleye, bass, pike, muskie

campgrounds.sub: Larger units · Own beach and boat ramp
Radio-free and pet-free loops · Shared beach

trails.name: Coureur des Bois Trail
trails.detail: Hilly forest loop named for the fur runners

trails.name: Outaouais Trail
trails.detail: Long loop through fields and woods along the river

trails.name: Wawashkeshi Trail
trails.detail: Short deer trail with one big hill at the start

name: Wabakimi
blurb: Canada's second largest wilderness park: two million acres of boreal canoe country and woodland caribou range north of Armstrong. Permits reservable, routes endless.
fishing: Walleye, pike, brook trout across a thousand lakes

name: Wakami Lake
blurb: A 15 km boreal lake on the Arctic watershed divide southeast of Chapleau. Ontario's songbird nursery, with the historic logging exhibit trail.
fishing: Wakami Lake: walleye, northern pike, whitefish

trails.name: Historic Logging Trail
trails.detail: Horse-to-machine logging history with the real equipment

name: Wasaga Beach
blurb: The world's longest freshwater beach, 14 km of sand on Nottawasaga Bay across eight beach areas. Day use only.
fishing: Nottawasaga River: salmon and rainbow runs, bass

trails.name: High Dunes Trail
trails.detail: Over the wooded dunes at the Wasaga Nordic Centre

name: Wheatley
blurb: The southernmost campground in Ontario. Carolinian forest and creek channels near Point Pelee; paddle the creeks, walk the Lake Erie beach.
fishing: Lake Erie: perch, bass, walleye

campgrounds.sub: Smallest · Surrounded by creeks · Footbridge to the beach

name: White Lake
blurb: A big warm lake off the Superior circle route near White River, birthplace of the real Winnie the Pooh. Pines, pull-throughs, and serious walleye.
fishing: White Lake: walleye, pike, whitefish

name: Windy Lake
blurb: Sandy beaches and clear swimming northwest of Sudbury, with walk-in shoreline sites and year-round yurts.
fishing: Windy Lake: lake trout, whitefish, perch

campgrounds.sub: 56 Electrical · 7 Walk-in radio-free on the shore · Yurts

name: Woodland Caribou
blurb: Granite canoe wilderness running to the Manitoba border, with ancient pictographs and more caribou than people. Backcountry permits only.
fishing: Walleye, pike, lake trout
